description_long: This is a traditional hammer featuring a robust wooden handle and a metallic head. The head is a classic claw design, with one side flat for striking and the other curved and split for nail removal. The wood of the handle appears aged, with a natural grain and some discoloration, suggesting frequent use. The metal head shows signs of oxidation and wear, with a dull, matte finish and some surface scratches. The overall construction is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. This hammer is typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or construction sites.
description: A well-used hammer with a wooden handle and a worn, metallic claw head, showing signs of age and frequent use.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from the side, clearly showing the curved claw and the flat striking face of the head, as well as the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Viewed from above, the circular cross-section of the handle and the width of the hammer head are visible, with the claw and striking face aligned horizontally.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile highlights the curvature of the claw and the straightness of the handle, with more detail on the head's wear.
description_view_3: From below, the rounded end of the handle and the underside of the hammer head are visible, emphasizing the thickness of the head and the handle's attachment.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with a natural finish, no visible ergonomic shaping or grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: claw hammer head with a flat striking face and a curved, split claw for nail removal, made of metal
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible surface scratches, discoloration, and oxidation on the metal head; handle shows signs of wear and aging
